The most important variable to monitor today is the 10-year yield. If we see a sustained break above 4.35 percent without a corresponding widening in the C.D.X. indices, the pressure on total return mandates will become unsustainable. The "peace dividend" is gone; now we see who was actually hedged for the "war tax."

That’s the view for this morning. Good luck out there.

Jefferies is the scrappy challenger in the investment banking world. They brought in ten point eight billion dollars in revenue last year and carry eighteen point one billion in debt. Unlike the massive money center banks, Jefferies is a pure-play investment bank and asset manager. This makes them more sensitive to capital market cycles. When M.A. activity is booming, they are a cash machine.

We have a situation where equities are pricing for perfection while the bond market is pricing for a regime shift. The key takeaway here is the disconnect in volatility; the V.I.X. is not yet reflecting the stress we see in the energy and power markets.

The single most important variable to watch today is the crude oil price reaction to the naval blockade news. If we see a sustained breakout there, expect the bear flattener in Treasuries to accelerate as the market prices in a Fed that is boxed in by stagflationary risks.

That’s the briefing for this morning. I’ll see you back here tomorrow.

We have a situation where the equity market is celebrating the removal of a tail risk, while the bond market is realizing that the removal of that risk also removes the Fed’s path to easing.
